Cao Yu (Pengzu)
曹宇 (彭祖)
Lived ADca.199–278

ThePrinceofYan[燕王] was[Cao] Yu, styledPengzu. In the sixteenth year of theera [211], he was appointed asMarquis of a Capital Township. In the twenty-second year [217], he changed fief toMarquisofLuyang[魯陽侯]. In the second year of theera [221], he advanced in peerage to becomeDuke. In the third year [222], he was made thePrinceofXiapi[下邳王]. In the fifth year, he changed fief to that ofShanfu county[單父縣]. In the sixth year of theera [232], he changed fief toPrinceofYan[燕王].
When theBrightEmperorwas young, he andCao Yuresided together, so he loved him and treated him differently. After he ascended the throne, the favor and gifts he gave were outstanding compared to the other princes. In the third year of theera [235], he [Cao Yu] was summoned to thecapital. In the first year of theera [237], he returned toYe[鄴]. It was summer in the second year [238] when he was summoned again to thecapital. It was winter in the twelfth month when theBrightEmperorbecame seriously ill, and conferred[Cao] YuasGrand General, entrusting him with future matters. Bearing the office for four days,[Cao] Yuthen firmly resigned. TheEmperorhad changed his mind as well, so he removed[Cao] Yufrom office. It was summer in the third year [239] when he returned toYe[鄴].
During the,anderas, the fief gradually increased, joining with the previous for a total of five thousand and five hundred households. TheTownship DukeofChangdao[常道鄉公],[Cao] Huan, was the son ofCao Yu, and he entered [the capital] to succeed in the main lineage of the clan.
